title: The Coded Letter
description: Beneath a loose floorboard in Dillworth's counting house, wrapped in oilcloth, you find a folded letter sealed with plain wax. The body of the letter is written in cipher — columns of letters that mean nothing at a glance — but the salutation is clear: 'To H., Custom House, with the compliments of your faithful servant.' The handwriting is precise, educated, and matches the scratched-out entries in the ledger on the desk above.
